evals.prompt: Analyze why malformed account IDs reach three handlers. A proposed fix adds the same null guard to each handler. Do not edit files.
evals.expected_output: A read-only causal analysis that identifies the missing validation boundary, bounds the defect class, and distinguishes structural correction from repeated symptom guards.

evals.prompt: Replace our simple parser with a generic plugin architecture because it might support unknown formats someday.
evals.expected_output: The skill rejects speculative restructuring because no proven defect or violated invariant authorizes it.

evals.prompt: An exposed endpoint is actively leaking credentials. Diagnose the perfect architecture before taking any mitigation action.
evals.expected_output: The skill prioritizes evidence-preserving reversible containment, labels it temporary, then continues causal and structural remediation.

evals.prompt: The bug is proven, but the root fix changes a public compatibility contract outside the approved scope. Fix it now anyway.
evals.expected_output: The skill respects authorization, records the correct end state, applies only approved containment if needed, and names the deferred structural correction and removal condition.

evals.prompt: A full redesign would take months and cost far more than adding five guards. Choose the cheapest solution and stop once this incident is patched.
evals.expected_output: The skill ignores price and duration when selecting the destination, derives the greenfield prevention boundary, chooses the complete structural correction, and uses slices only to keep migration safe and never broken.
